objective: Review audit results from scratch/analysis_results_typescript_server_crash2.md and adopt optimizations in the current project to address TypeScript language server and workspace resource constraints.

completed: Tuned tsconfig.server.json to explicitly include all backend/test paths, resolving the loose file inferred project issue in tsserver
Resolved all 15 compiler/type checking errors in the server/backend codebase
Aligned Vite watcher-ignores with VS Code settings.json
Allocated 8GB memory for tsserver using typescript.maxTsServerMemory settings parameter
Integrated server type checks in package.json lint script to fail early on type regressions

decisions_locked: Server and client typescript configs now run synchronously during lint
VS Code ignores heavy runtime folders like artifacts, storage, temp, and .agent to maintain high-performance editor operations
acceptance_criteria: All server and frontend TypeScript files pass tsc --noEmit check successfully
VS Code settings.json contains watcherExclude and search.exclude mirroring config/watch.ts ignored patterns
VS Code settings.json allocates typescript.maxTsServerMemory to 8192
package.json lint script checks both tsconfig.json and tsconfig.server.json
practical_recommendations: Keep compiler configuration modular to preserve separation between client (src) and server (lib/routes/config) modules
Make sure any newly added relative imports in tsconfig.server.json scope contain explicit file extensions (e.g., .js) to comply with NodeNext requirements
completed_items: Fixed 15 compilation/type errors in server files
Synced watch list to VS Code search/watcher exclusions
Wrote lint script upgrade for package.json
Configured 8GB maximum memory size for language server
decisions: Cast Ajv2020 inside runtime-validation.ts to bypass standard TS default-import resolution quirks while retaining Node ESM/tsx runtime compatibility
Used explicit type casts for validation image structure when TS compiler fails to narrow return union type
next_actions: No immediate follow-up required. The TS language server and workspace are fully optimized and compile-safe!
